media.info: Media and technology are the central nervous system of globalization, allowing instantaneous communication and the spread of culture and information across borders.

markers.title: Silicon Valley, USA
markers.desc: The global hub for high technology and innovation, home to the world's largest tech giants like Apple, Google, and Meta.

markers.title: Washington D.C., USA
markers.desc: The seat of federal regulation, defining internet policy, antitrust laws, and data privacy legislation for global tech networks.

markers.title: London, UK
markers.desc: Europe's leading center for creative industries, global broadcasting networks, and telecom nodes.

markers.title: Bengaluru, India
markers.desc: Known as the Silicon Valley of India, it is a leading global IT cluster and startup hub driving tech services worldwide.

markers.title: Tokyo, Japan
markers.desc: A global powerhouse in hardware innovation, consumer electronics, and high-speed fiber infrastructure.

cities.info: Global cities act as primary nodes in the global economic network, serving as hubs for finance, culture, and international relations.

markers.title: New York City, USA
markers.desc: The premier global financial capital, hosting the UN Headquarters, Wall Street, and central hubs of global media and commerce.

markers.title: Paris, France
markers.desc: A leading global capital of culture, fashion, international diplomacy, and tourism.

markers.title: Dubai, UAE
markers.desc: A hyper-modern global city and trade crossroads, linking East and West through massive transport, real estate, and financial networks.

markers.title: Mumbai, India
markers.desc: India's financial capital, housing the Bombay Stock Exchange and Bollywood, acting as a massive economic anchor in South Asia.

markers.title: Shanghai, China
markers.desc: A powerhouse global city boasting the world's busiest container port, symbol of China's economic rise and industrial integration.

markers.title: São Paulo, Brazil
markers.desc: The largest city in the Southern Hemisphere, serving as the financial engine of South America and a major industrial hub.

migration.info: Migration shapes the contemporary world by blending cultures, driving economic shifts, and creating transnational communities.

markers.title: Border Entry, United States
markers.desc: A primary global migration destination, managing significant visa programs, labor integration, and complex border policy reforms.

markers.title: Brussels, Belgium
markers.desc: The administrative heart of the European Union, formulating refugee integration policies and Schengen Zone rules.

markers.title: Canberra, Australia
markers.desc: The federal capital managing skilled migration programs and humanitarian intakes.

markers.title: Mexico City, Mexico
markers.desc: A major transit hub for migratory flows connecting Central America to North America.

markers.title: Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
markers.desc: A major capital city hosting millions of migrant workers from South and Southeast Asia.

markers.title: Zuma Rock, Abuja (Nigeria)
markers.desc: The magnificent 725-metre high natural monolith near Abuja, featured on the 100 Naira banknote and acting as the majestic geographic gateway to Nigeria's capital.

food.info: Global food security relies on a complex web of agricultural production, international trade, and climate stability. Unequal distribution remains a critical challenge.

markers.title: United States Capitol, Washington D.C.
markers.desc: Where critical agricultural subsidies, US farm bills, and international food aid programs are signed into law.

markers.title: Brasília, Brazil
markers.desc: The federal seat directing agricultural initiatives, Amazon conservation policies, and soybean export trade.

markers.title: Kyiv, Ukraine
markers.desc: The agricultural capital center of Europe's breadbasket, directing critical grain and sunflower oil supplies worldwide.

markers.title: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia
markers.desc: The capital of East Africa's diplomatic hub, hosting foreign aid coordination centers for Horn of Africa food security.

markers.title: Sana'a, Yemen
markers.desc: A capital city under siege, facing severe humanitarian and supply-chain food security crises.
